sections.paragraph:
Ἰτουραῖος, αία, αῖον (since Eupolemus [II b.c.] 723 Fgm. 2 Jac. [in Eus., PE 9, 30, 3], mostly in the pl. of the subst. masc. Ἰτουραῖοι [Appian, Mithrid. 106 §499, Bell. Civ. 5, 7, §31; Arrian, Alans 1 and 18; Jos., Ant. 13, 318]. Schürer 561–67) ἡ Ἰ. χώρα Ituraea, a region along the Lebanon and Anti-Lebanon ranges belonging to the Tetrarchy of Philip, w. Chalcis as its capital city Lk 3:1. S. Schürer loc. cit. for sources and lit.—M-M.

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

  1. pl. = plural

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

masc. masc. = masculine

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

loc. cit loc. cit = loco citato (in the place already cited)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 485
